description: Direct income support to all farmers holding cultivable land

rules.description: Must be engaged in farming
Must hold at least some cultivable land
Must have an active bank account for DBT
Aadhaar number required for authentication
Target is small and marginal farmers; landless agricultural workers excluded

ambiguity_flags.description: Guidelines say 'land holding farmers' but ambiguous whether leased/mortgaged land counts. State revenue records vary.
Scheme excludes 'high-income farmers' and 'govt employees' but threshold and definitions vary by state
Some guidelines mention family income limits but definition of 'family' (joint vs nuclear) unclear

scheme_name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
description: Demand-based rural employment guarantee; 100 days work per financial year

rules.description: Must reside in rural area (as per Census 2011)
Must be at least 18 years old
Must be Indian citizen or documented long-term resident

ambiguity_flags.description: Census 2011 definition used, but urban sprawl makes boundaries fuzzy in many states
Demand-based but state capacity varies; actual job availability not guaranteed across all regions
Guidelines exclude 'govt employees' but definition of casual/contractual workers varies
Can MGNREGA beneficiary also claim PM Kisan simultaneously? Depends on state policy.

scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat - PM-JAY (Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana)
description: Health insurance for vulnerable families; ₹5 lakh annual coverage

rules.description: Must be in SECC 2011 deprived categories (D1=Agricultural, D2=Non-agricultural landless)
BPL status strongly indicates eligibility (alternate pathway to SECC check)
Income ceiling for APL (Above Poverty Line) families in rural areas

ambiguity_flags.description: SECC 2011 is now 13+ years old; many eligible people not in database. States use different supplementary lists.
BPL card holders may not be in SECC database and vice versa. Reconciliation rules differ by state.
Urban and rural income limits differ; state-level modifications not consistently applied
Definition of 'family' (nuclear vs joint vs household) for income calculation unclear

description: Provides assistance for construction of pucca houses in rural areas

rules.description: Must be in rural area as per Census 2011
Applicant must not own a pucca house
BPL status strongly preferred; APL families also eligible based on state norms
Income ceiling for rural areas (varies by state)

ambiguity_flags.description: Guidelines unclear on what constitutes 'pucca' — cement vs concrete vs brick standards vary by state
Central guideline ₹1.75L but states modify; check state notification

description: Assists urban poor in accessing housing credit-linked subsidy

rules.description: Must be in urban area (City/town as per Census 2011)
Applicant and family members must not own any pucca house
Must fall in EWS/LIG/MIG income slab

ambiguity_flags.issue: Income slabs vary by city tier
ambiguity_flags.description: EWS/LIG/MIG income limits differ by metro vs tier-2 vs tier-3 cities
Interest subsidy depends on loan amount and repayment period; calculations vary

benefit_amount: ₹2.67-4.5L subsidy (depends on income slab and loan)

description: Free LPG connection for BPL and Below Poverty Line households

rules.description: Must have BPL card or be eligible under BPL criteria
Aadhaar required for authentication and DBT of subsidy
Applicant must not have existing LPG connection

ambiguity_flags.description: Central BPL card issue stopped in 2015; many states use supplementary lists
Unclear if both spouses can apply or only one connection per household

description: Universal banking scheme to provide bank account access to all citizens

rules.description: Must be Indian citizen
Applicant must not have an existing bank account in any bank

ambiguity_flags.description: Unclear if inactive accounts or joint accounts count; varies by bank implementation

benefit_amount: Bank account with ₹1,00,000 OD limit + life insurance

description: Life insurance scheme for low-income families; ₹2L accidental death coverage

rules.description: Minimum age 18 years
Maximum age 70 years
Must have savings/current account for premium auto-debit

ambiguity_flags.description: Auto-debit on 1st of month; if account balance insufficient, eligibility lapses automatically

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
description: Life insurance scheme covering death from any cause

rules.description: Minimum age 18 years
Maximum age 50 years (different from Suraksha Bima)
Must have savings account for auto-debit

ambiguity_flags.description: Both schemes target low-income; unclear if beneficiary can hold both simultaneously

description: Pension scheme for unorganized sector workers; guaranteed minimum pension from age 60

rules.description: Entry age minimum 18 years
Entry age maximum 40 years (20-year contribution period to age 60)
Must have savings account for contribution auto-debit

ambiguity_flags.description: Pension varies by contribution amount; minimum guaranteed but complex calculation

benefit_amount: Minimum ₹1000-5000/month pension from age 60 (depends on contribution)

description: Provides scholarships to meritorious students from economically weaker sections

rules.description: Must be enrolled in government or private school/college
Family annual income ≤ ₹2.5 lakh
Post-matric scholarships for age 18+

ambiguity_flags.description: Scholarship eligibility depends on both academic merit and EWS status; scoring mechanism unclear

description: Cash assistance for pregnant and lactating women; ₹5000 per trimester

rules.description: Applicant must be woman
Must be pregnant or lactating mother
Benefit applicable only for first live birth

ambiguity_flags.description: Unclear if previous miscarriage/stillbirth resets counter; remarriage status ambiguous
Unclear if adoptive mothers or surrogate mothers qualify

benefit_amount: ₹5000 per trimester (₹1000 at registration, ₹2000 each trimester)

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Poshan Shakti Nirman (PM POSHAN)
description: Mid-day meal scheme for school children; free nutritious meal

rules.description: Must be enrolled in government or government-aided school
Minimum age 6 years (Class I start)
Maximum age 14 years (up to Class VIII)

ambiguity_flags.description: No central verification; relies on school certificate; definitions of govt/aided vary by state

benefit_amount: Free nutritious meal during school days (no direct cash)

description: Business loan for SC/ST entrepreneurs or women entrepreneurs; ₹10L-1Cr

rules.description: Women entrepreneurs eligible (fast-track)
SC/ST entrepreneurs eligible (separate category)
Net worth/annual income limit for business applicants

ambiguity_flags.issue: Woman AND SC/ST: which gets priority?
ambiguity_flags.description: Unclear if woman SC/ST entrepreneur has precedence or if categories are mutually exclusive
Loan approval depends on viability of business plan; criteria vary by bank

scheme_name: PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi)
description: Working capital loan for street vendors; ₹50,000 unsecured

rules.description: Must be engaged in street vending
Must have pre-COVID vending certificate from Municipal Corporation
Must have bank account for loan disbursement

ambiguity_flags.description: Many street vendors never had formal certificates; vending list maintenance varies by ULB
Unclear if e-commerce or online vendors qualify; scheme designed for traditional street traders

benefit_amount: Unsecured working capital loan ₹50,000 at 7% interest with CGTMSE guarantee
